translationPolicy: Arabic candidates are design-layer terminology proposals. They do not create missing paragraph translations and are not approved for Student Mode until terminologyApprovalStatus is approved.

termSchema.optionalFieldsNote: explanatoryGlosses holds descriptive paraphrases (e.g., a definitional gloss) that aid intuition but are not fully equivalent alternate terms; keep these distinct from alternateArabicTerms, which lists true interchangeable synonyms.

terms.definition: A scalar measure of path length or spatial extent; the precise usage must be stated.
terms.usageNotes: Do not use cumulative distance when a signed position coordinate or displacement is intended.
discouragedTranslations: الموضع when path length is meant
terms.reviewBasis: Approved by the project owner based on Claude's completed terminology review of ch01-term-distance (see conversation record). This records the project owner's approval decision, not an independently derived linguistic re-verification beyond that review. Extended to ch01-t01 on 2026-07-17 (topicIds only; Arabic term, definition, and source lineage unchanged) based on the same reasoning, per docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_READINESS_GLOSSARY_DECISION_BRIEF.md and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_GLOSSARY_INVENTORY_RECONCILIATION_ADDENDUM.md.

terms.definition: An object's location relative to a chosen origin and coordinate direction.
terms.usageNotes: Prefer الموضع in formal kinematics; state the reference origin.
discouragedTranslations: المسافة when a signed coordinate is meant

terms.definition: The vector change in position from an initial point to a final point.
terms.usageNotes: Average velocity uses displacement, not total path length.

terms.definition: A scalar rate at which distance is accumulated over time.
terms.usageNotes: Qualify average versus instantaneous speed; in ch01-t01, this term specifically denotes average speed over a stated distance and time interval (v = d/t). Speed is a scalar quantity (كمية قياسية) and is a derived quantity (الكمية المشتقة) built from distance (المسافة) divided by time — it is not itself fundamental.
terms.reviewBasis: Approved by the project owner for ch01-t01 based on Claude's completed terminology review and reconciliation (see docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_READINESS_GLOSSARY_DECISION_BRIEF.md and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_GLOSSARY_INVENTORY_RECONCILIATION_ADDENDUM.md). S3-P003 was added to sourceReferences as the ch01-t01-specific evidentiary basis (the derived-quantity/L-T illustrative example); the pre-existing S2-SEG102/SCA06/SCA07 references are preserved unchanged as lineage relevant to other, not-yet-authorized topics. This records the project owner's approval decision, not an independently derived linguistic re-verification beyond that review.

terms.definition: A physical quantity defined independently rather than expressed as a combination of other physical quantities. In this chapter's mechanics content, distance, time, and mass are the relevant examples.
terms.usageNotes: Contrast with derived quantity (الكمية المشتقة): a derived quantity, such as speed, is formed by combining fundamental quantities. Dimensional symbols such as L (length), T (time), and M (mass) represent dimensions, not measured-variable symbols — do not confuse them with the equation variables d (measured distance) or t (elapsed time). When explaining a fundamental quantity's dimensional symbol, use البُعد for 'dimension' (the standard Arabic dimensional-analysis term, compare التحليل البعدي); do not substitute المسافة (distance), even though 'بُعد' has an unrelated everyday sense connected to remoteness/distance in casual Arabic.
discouragedTranslations: المسافة for 'dimension' (بُعد) when the dimensional-symbol sense is meant — a false-friend risk, not a synonym
terms.reviewBasis: Approved by the project owner based on Claude's completed terminology review and reconciliation (see docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_READINESS_GLOSSARY_DECISION_BRIEF.md and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_GLOSSARY_INVENTORY_RECONCILIATION_ADDENDUM.md). No separate glossary term was created for 'dimension'/'dimensional notation' — that narrow, single-context concept is deliberately folded into this term's own usageNotes rather than given its own record, per the reconciliation addendum's explicit resolution. This records the project owner's approval decision, not an independently derived linguistic re-verification beyond that review.

terms.definition: A physical quantity expressed using one or more fundamental quantities, combined through multiplication and/or division, without an independent unit of its own. Speed is a derived quantity because it combines distance and time; speed has dimension L/T.
terms.usageNotes: Contrast with fundamental quantity (الكمية الأساسية). Do not present a derived quantity as if it required its own separate fundamental unit.
terms.reviewBasis: Approved by the project owner based on Claude's completed terminology review and reconciliation (see docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_READINESS_GLOSSARY_DECISION_BRIEF.md and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_GLOSSARY_INVENTORY_RECONCILIATION_ADDENDUM.md). This records the project owner's approval decision, not an independently derived linguistic re-verification beyond that review.

terms.definition: The vector rate of change of position.
terms.usageNotes: Always preserve direction or sign convention.
discouragedTranslations: السرعة without direction when a vector is meant
terms.reviewBasis: Approved by the project owner based on Claude's completed terminology review of ch01-term-velocity (see conversation record). This records the project owner's approval decision, not an independently derived linguistic re-verification beyond that review.

terms.definition: The vector rate of change of velocity.
terms.usageNotes: A change of direction is acceleration even when speed is constant. In signed kinematics, the sign of acceleration depends on the chosen positive-axis convention (see ch01-corr-007) and does not by itself mean the object is slowing down or reversing direction; whether it does depends on the sign of velocity at that same instant.
discouragedTranslations: زيادة السرعة as a complete definition
terms.reviewBasis: Approved by the project owner based on Claude's completed terminology review of ch01-term-acceleration (see conversation record). This records the project owner's approval decision, not an independently derived linguistic re-verification beyond that review. Extended to ch01-t04 on 2026-07-17 (topicIds only; Arabic term, definition, and source lineage unchanged) because ch01-t04's W = mg equation reuses the exact |g| ≈ 9.8 m/s^2 value already established here by ch01-corr-007, per docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_READINESS_GLOSSARY_DECISION_BRIEF.md and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_GLOSSARY_INVENTORY_RECONCILIATION_ADDENDUM.md.

terms.definition: A measure of an object's inertia.
terms.usageNotes: Amount of matter may be used only as introductory intuition, not the primary operational definition. Distinguish from weight (الوزن): mass does not depend on location, while gravitational weight does.
terms.reviewBasis: Approved by the project owner based on Claude's completed terminology review and reconciliation (see docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_READINESS_GLOSSARY_DECISION_BRIEF.md and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_GLOSSARY_INVENTORY_RECONCILIATION_ADDENDUM.md). This records the project owner's approval decision, not an independently derived linguistic re-verification beyond that review.

terms.definition: The gravitational force acting on an object in the stated context.
terms.usageNotes: Distinguish gravitational weight from apparent weight (الوزن الظاهري) and from mass (الكتلة). Weight is a force (W = mg where relevant) and is properly expressed in newtons, not kilograms.
terms.reviewBasis: Approved by the project owner based on Claude's completed terminology review and reconciliation (see docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_READINESS_GLOSSARY_DECISION_BRIEF.md and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_GLOSSARY_INVENTORY_RECONCILIATION_ADDENDUM.md). الوزن الحقيقي is deliberately not used anywhere in this record as a contrast label for apparent weight. This records the project owner's approval decision, not an independently derived linguistic re-verification beyond that review.

terms.definition: The support-force magnitude indicated by a scale or experienced by a body.
terms.usageNotes: It can differ from gravitational weight during acceleration; in this chapter's introductory, non-accelerating treatment, apparent weight equals gravitational weight only in an ordinary, static/resting situation. 'Apparent weight' (الوزن الظاهري) is project-authored, English-language interpretive terminology, per the qualification recorded in ch01-corr-002's approval — it is not presented as a verbatim quotation from any audited source, and this same distinction should be carried into its Arabic usage. Apparent weight is not 'fake' or illusory weight: it is a real, measurable support force, distinct from — not less real than — gravitational weight (W = mg). قراءة الميزان ("the scale reading") may be used as a concrete, supporting explanatory phrase, especially on first introduction.
discouragedTranslations: الوزن الحقيقي — wrongly implies apparent weight is not a real or true force; not to be used as the preferred contrast term
terms.reviewBasis: Approved WITH REVISION by the project owner based on Claude's completed terminology review and reconciliation (see docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_READINESS_GLOSSARY_DECISION_BRIEF.md and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_GLOSSARY_INVENTORY_RECONCILIATION_ADDENDUM.md). The revision adds an explicit usage note distinguishing apparent weight from gravitational weight and from a normal/scale-reading force, framing the term as project-authored interpretive terminology consistent with ch01-corr-002's own approved qualification, and adding a misconception warning that apparent weight is not 'fake' weight. This records the project owner's approval decision, not an independently derived linguistic re-verification beyond that review.

terms.definition: A quantity specified by magnitude and unit without a direction.
terms.usageNotes: Speed, mass, and time are scalar quantities in this chapter.
discouragedTranslations: كمية عددية when it obscures the physics distinction
terms.reviewBasis: Approved by the project owner for ch01-t01 based on Claude's completed terminology review and reconciliation (see docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_READINESS_GLOSSARY_DECISION_BRIEF.md and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_GLOSSARY_INVENTORY_RECONCILIATION_ADDENDUM.md). This records the project owner's approval decision, not an independently derived linguistic re-verification beyond that review.

terms.definition: A quantity specified by magnitude and direction.
terms.usageNotes: The arrow representation must preserve both length scale and direction.

terms.definition: The single vector equal to the vector sum of two or more vectors.
terms.usageNotes: Do not infer its direction from magnitudes alone.

terms.definition: The change in a graph's vertical coordinate divided by the change in its horizontal coordinate.
terms.usageNotes: Name both axes before assigning physical meaning.
discouragedTranslations: السرعة unless the graph is explicitly position versus time

terms.definition: A description of how a graph's slope changes as the horizontal variable increases.
terms.usageNotes: Use concave up or concave down; do not substitute the vague phrase curves upward.

terms.definition: Motion modeled with gravity as the only force acting.
terms.usageNotes: State the air-resistance approximation and coordinate sign convention.
terms.reviewBasis: Approved by the project owner based on Claude's completed terminology review of ch01-term-free-fall (see conversation record). This records the project owner's approval decision, not an independently derived linguistic re-verification beyond that review.

terms.definition: The inward acceleration associated with the continuous change of velocity direction in circular motion.
terms.usageNotes: Velocity is tangent to the path; acceleration points toward the center.
terms.reviewBasis: Approved by the project owner based on Claude's completed terminology review of ch01-term-centripetal-acceleration (see conversation record). This records the project owner's approval decision, not an independently derived linguistic re-verification beyond that review.

terms.definition: The time required for one complete cycle of a specified periodic process.
terms.usageNotes: Always name the periodic process and state what counts as one complete cycle before using period; do not assume any repeated motion is automatically periodic. "الدور" has common non-physics meanings (e.g., "turn," or a building's "floor"); "زمن الدورة" may be used when first introducing the concept to beginners to avoid this ambiguity.
terms.reviewBasis: Approved by the project owner based on Claude's completed terminology review of ch01-term-period (see conversation record). This records the project owner's approval decision, not an independently derived linguistic re-verification beyond that review.

terms.definition: The number of complete cycles of a specified periodic process per unit time.
terms.usageNotes: Frequency is the reciprocal of period only once one complete cycle has been defined for the process in question; do not count a single back-and-forth swing as more than one cycle unless explicitly defined that way. "عدد الدورات في الثانية" ("number of cycles per second") is retained as an explanatory teaching gloss for building intuition, not as a fully equivalent alternate term for "التردد."
terms.reviewBasis: Approved by the project owner based on Claude's completed terminology review of ch01-term-frequency (see conversation record). This records the project owner's approval decision, not an independently derived linguistic re-verification beyond that review.
